persons.arguments_against: The dossier raises the single-initial namesake warning, but the merge does not rest on the name: the Chief Justiceship of New Zealand is a unique office held by one person at a time; the biography dates his tenure 'chief justice of N.Z., 1899-1926', and every one of the stint's fifteen records (1900-1922) is 'Chief Justice' in the Judicial department, wholly contained in that window; every record carries K.C.M.G., matching the biography's K.C.M.G. (1886). A same-name relative (e.g. a son — Stout had sons in NZ law) could not simultaneously be Chief Justice, and only 2 Stouts exist in the entire staff-list corpus. The only way this fails is if the biography's own chief-justiceship dates are wrong, for which there is no indication across 27 editions.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence that a different Robert Stout held the New Zealand Chief Justiceship during 1900-1922, contradicting the biography's stated 1899-1926 tenure.

notes: All deterministic checks pass, position_sim at 100, and 15 strict edition co-occurrences. Unique judicial office with exact tenure containment makes this one of the strongest possible matches.
